Title: Autophagy regulates glucose-mediated root meristem activity by modulating ROS production in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls root meristem activity and primary root growth under glucose stress
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0%, 1%, 3%, or 5% glucose; IBA; GSH; AsA; NPA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Autophagy mediated by ATG8E promotes glucose-triggered peroxisome degradation and supports ROS accumulation that suppresses root meristem activity under high glucose
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutive autophagosome formation in root meristem zone, elongation zone, and differentiation zone; transiently suppressed by short-term high glucose
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GFP-ATG8E autophagosome imaging; free GFP cleavage assay; Y2H; co-immunoprecipitation with ABCD1-B; mutant phenotyping; ROS staining; peroxisome marker analysis
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GFP-ATG8E marker promoter context not specified; HA/FLAG transient constructs in protoplasts for CoIP
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Autophagy deficiency causes shorter roots under sugar deprivation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating autophagy may tune root growth responses to sugar status, but loss of autophagy impairs growth under carbon starvation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. High glucose transiently suppresses constitutive ATG8E-marked autophagy in root tips. 2. ATG8E physically interacts with ABCD1 Walker B motif, linking autophagy to peroxisome regulation. 3. Functional autophagy is required for glucose-promoted peroxisome degradation and for full ROS-mediated inhibition of root meristem activity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ATG8E was used as the principal autophagosome marker and mechanistic interactor in the study; core autophagy mutants analyzed were atg2-1, atg5-1, and atg7-3.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: auxin (IAA) via IBA β-oxidation; glucose-induced ROS
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains root meristem activity and root growth responses under elevated glucose
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Peroxisomal ATP-binding cassette transporter ABCD family; PXA1/COMATOSE
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABCD1 overexpression increases sensitivity to high glucose and elevates ROS; in atg mutants it partially rescues the glucose-tolerant, low-ROS phenotype
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression (35S:ABCD1); genetic crosses with atg5-1 and atg7-3; root phenotyping under glucose/IBA; DAB/NBT ROS staining; Y2H and CoIP interaction with ATG8E
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ABCD1 overexpression causes hypersensitivity to elevated glucose and IBA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ABCD1 modulation could adjust peroxisome-dependent ROS/auxin balance in roots, but overexpression may reduce root growth under high sugar
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABCD1 physically interacts with ATG8E through the Walker B motif region. 2. ABCD1 overexpression restores ROS accumulation and partially rescues the glucose-associated root phenotype of autophagy mutants. 3. ABCD1 links peroxisomal β-oxidation/auxin precursor metabolism with autophagy-mediated glucose responses.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Formerly PXA1/peroxisomal ABC transporter 1; acts as a multifunctional peroxisomal transporter rather than a biosynthetic enzyme or TF.
